name: Conductor
description: macOS desktop app for running parallel Claude Code and Codex agents in isolated git worktrees, with a unified dashboard for reviewing and merging their changes.

description.value: Mac desktop harness that runs multiple Claude Code / Codex agents in parallel across isolated git worktrees with a unified diff-review and merge UI.

repository.comment: Closed-source proprietary app; no public source repository.

creator.comment: YC-backed startup; raised a reported $22M Series A.

latest-release.comment: v0.48.5 released 2026-04-18 per changelog; Homebrew cask at same version as of research date.

license.comment: Proprietary macOS binary; no source available.

pricing-model.comment: App itself is free to download and use as of 2026-04; users bring their own Claude/OpenAI credentials and pay underlying API/subscription costs. No paid Conductor tier exists yet, but the company is commercial (Series A) — closest existing tag is freemium.

covered-phases.comment: Todos feature gates merge (tasks); agents execute work (implementation); diff viewer + PR-style comments cover review. No dedicated spec, architecture, planning, test, or deploy phases in the workflow.

methodology-style.comment: Value is primarily the tooling that runs and isolates multiple concurrent agents; no prescriptive phase model.

opinionated.comment: No mandated phase flow — the tool provides workspaces, checkpoints, and review UI but lets the user drive the workflow.

ceremony-level.comment: Minimal process: pick a repo, spawn agents, review diffs, merge. No required artifacts beyond optional todos.

mechanization-level.comment: App orchestrates worktree creation, branch management, setup scripts, and agent execution automatically; user interaction is GUI-driven rather than prompt copy-paste.

hitl-checkpoints.comment: Todos block merging until checked off; diff review happens in-app before merge.

approval-gates.comment: Implementation output is gated by todos + manual merge. No explicit spec/plan/deploy gates.

autonomous-e2e.comment: Merge to the main branch requires a human action in the UI; no configuration ships that skips it.

autonomy-level.comment: Default configuration: agents run autonomously inside a workspace, but merging back requires human diff review and todo sign-off — phase-level approval.

rollback-support.comment: Checkpoints store per-turn snapshots in a private git ref (.git/refs/conductor-checkpoints/) and expose a revert UI.

supported-agents.comment: First-party support only for Claude Code and OpenAI Codex CLI; no documented Cursor/Aider/Windsurf integration.

editor-integrations.comment: Conductor is a standalone macOS GUI app with its own editor-like UI and integrated terminal — none of the predefined tags (vscode/jetbrains/neovim/terminal-only/web) apply.

model-agnostic.comment: Locked to the Claude Code and Codex agent runtimes; cannot swap in an arbitrary agent. Underlying model choice (e.g., Opus 4.7, GPT-5.3-Codex) is configurable, and gateway providers (Bedrock, Vertex, OpenRouter) are supported, but the harness is vendor-specific.

mcp-support.comment: MCP listed as a core feature in the docs index for connecting external tools and data sources.

artifact-formats.comment: conductor.json for repo config; markdown for agent instructions (CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md-style), scratchpads, and todos.

artifacts-covered.comment: Todos are the only structured workflow artifact produced. Conductor does not generate specs, plans, architecture docs, or ADRs.

project-layout.value: conductor.json at the repo root (setup/run/archive scripts) plus optional agent instructions configured per-repo; agent workspaces live in git worktrees managed by the app.

artifacts-in-git.comment: Only conductor.json lives in the repo. Chat history, checkpoints (private git ref), scratchpads, and per-workspace todos are local-only and not shared via git.

language-agnostic.comment: No language lock-in; quickstart templates are provided for Django, Next.js, Phoenix, and Rails but any stack works.

enforcement-mechanisms.comment: Manual approval via todos/diff review gates merges. Checkpointing is implemented via Claude Code hooks; user-defined team/personal hooks are documented. No CI-check or agent-review enforcement.

strict-phase-ordering.comment: No prescribed phase sequence is enforced; users can drive agents freely.

artifact-validation.comment: No documented schema validation for produced artifacts (todos, chat, scratchpads). conductor.json has a known shape but no published JSON Schema validator.

memory-implementation.comment: Persistent context via per-repo instructions (CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md-equivalent) and per-workspace scratchpads. Chat history is persisted locally but scoped to the workspace/session.

persistent-memory.comment: Chat history and checkpoints persist on disk across app restarts; repo-level instructions persist in conductor.json.

project-context-files.comment: Repo-level 'instructions' configured via Repository Settings are the CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md analogue and are honored by agents at startup.

cost-tracking.comment: No Conductor-level token or cost reporting surfaced in docs or changelog as of 2026-04. Underlying Claude Code /cost works within the embedded agent but is not aggregated by Conductor.

run-history.comment: Per-turn checkpoints and full chat history per workspace are retained for review.

telemetry-opt.comment: Analytics (PostHog) are disclosed; user-level opt-out via Settings > Privacy, repo-level via enterpriseDataPrivacy=true in conductor.json.

custom-templates.comment: No user-defined artifact/prompt template system documented. Framework 'quickstarts' (Django, Next.js, Phoenix, Rails) are authored by Conductor, not user-extensible.

hooks.comment: Conductor uses Claude Code hooks for checkpointing and documents team/personal hook scripts; setup/run/archive scripts in conductor.json are additional lifecycle events.

sub-agents.comment: Conductor itself does not ship a user-defined persona or sub-agent system; any such behavior comes from the underlying Claude Code / Codex runtime and is not a Conductor workflow primitive.

marketplace.comment: No public marketplace or community-shared template repository documented.

install-mechanism.comment: Direct .dmg download from conductor.build; also available via `brew install --cask conductor`.

setup-time.comment: App install is fast, but first run requires GitHub auth, Claude Code/Codex credentials, workspace setup script, and dependency installs — typically 5-30 minutes end-to-end depending on the project.

self-hosted.comment: Runs entirely on the user's Mac; chat history and checkpoints stored locally. Account metadata is stored in Conductor's backend but the workflow itself executes locally.

requires-account.comment: Requires Claude and/or OpenAI credentials to drive the bundled agents, GitHub login for repo access, and a Conductor account (account data persisted to Conductor-managed Postgres).

git-worktree-support.comment: Worktrees are the core isolation primitive — every agent workspace is a managed worktree + branch.

documentation-quality.comment: Well-structured docs.conductor.build with dedicated sections for core concepts, guides, privacy, and a detailed versioned changelog. Not exhaustive (some features like cost tracking absent), hence 4 rather than 5.

github-stars.comment: Closed-source commercial product — no public GitHub repository to measure.

active-maintenance.comment: v0.48.5 released 2026-04-18; weekly release cadence visible in the changelog — well within the 90-day threshold as of 2026-04-21.

example-projects.comment: Framework quickstarts (Django, Next.js + Vercel, Elixir + Phoenix, Rails) ship in the docs; homepage lists customer testimonials from Linear, Vercel, Notion, Ramp, Life360, Square, Reducto, Spotify.

community-channel.comment: No public Discord/Slack/forum surfaced in docs, website, or Y Combinator page as of research date.
